name: Retrieve questions from a knowledge base with HTTP / REST API on autopilot for Document Search

parameters.content:
### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf06954-document`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: List Changed Docs (API)

### List Changed Docs (API)

GET https://api.example.com/v1/documents — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Ports: ①→ Remove Duplicate Documents · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Remove Duplicate Documents

Drops duplicates within this run (compared on `docId`).

Next: Attach Chunk Metadata

### Attach Chunk Metadata

Custom JavaScript: Attach chunk-level metadata before embedding

Next: Insert into Vector Store

### Insert into Vector Store

Writes embedded chunks into the vector index.

Ports: ①→ Count Indexed Chunks · ②→ Capture Failure Context

⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Pinecone API key

### Prepare Documents

Wraps incoming items as documents (with metadata) for the vector store.

Feeds **Insert into Vector Store** as `document`

### Chunk with Overlap

Chunks documents (~1200 chars, 120 overlap) so retrieval hits stay focused.

Feeds **Prepare Documents** as `textSplitter`

### Document Embeddings

Embedding model — MUST be the same model for indexing and querying, or retrieval quality collapses.

Feeds **Insert into Vector Store** as `embedding`

🔑 OpenAI API key

### Count Indexed Chunks

Collapses all items into a single summary item (for counts and run stats).

Next: Guard Non-Empty title

### Guard Non-Empty title

Drops items that fail the condition; only qualifying items continue (no false branch).

Next: Tag Pipeline Metadata

### Tag Pipeline Metadata

Shapes the item: writes `pipeline`, `version`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Send Summary to Slack

### Send Summary to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#knowledge`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#knowledge`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

# Retrieve questions from a knowledge base with HTTP / REST API on autopilot for Document Search

**Pattern:** RAG Pipeline · **Level:** Core · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Retrieve questions from a knowledge base with HTTP / REST API on autopilot for Document Search. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual document search is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → List Changed Docs (API) → Remove Duplicate Documents → Attach Chunk Metadata → Insert into Vector Store → Count Indexed Chunks → Guard Non-Empty title → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to rag pipeline without manual effort
- Anyone automating document search in ai
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Keeps your systems in sync
- Runs 24/7 in the background
- Standardizes the process every time

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; OpenAI API key; Pinecone API key; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Ingestion is a separate workflow from querying because indexing and answering have different triggers, schedules, and failure modes — a slow re-index should never block a live user question. Chunking with overlap (not whole documents) keeps each retrieval hit focused on relevant text instead of dragging in an entire document's unrelated sections.

## Practice exercises

Once it's wired up with real credentials, try:

1. Change chunkSize and chunkOverlap, then compare retrieval quality back in the query workflow.
2. Add a second file source and normalize both into the same chunk-metadata shape.
3. Swap the embedding model — but change it in both this workflow and the query workflow, since they must match.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🤖 AI cluster

Purple nodes form one AI unit: the root node orchestrates, sub-nodes plug in via typed ports (model / memory / tools / parser). Swap the chat model without rewiring anything else. Costs scale with tokens — keep prompts lean and cap iterations.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your document search process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
